violations.text: Chicken and Beef was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025
The sanitizer motor located on the Mechanical dish washer was not in good repair. Equipment must be maintained in good condition. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2025
The grout throughout the kitchen area was observed is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2025

Cheese was observed held on ice in a non working prep unit. Prep unit was measured at 60 DF. Cheese was observed at 60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cheese was voluntarily disposed of. All food items were moved to working unit. Establishment has new unit on order. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on prep unit, and grill draws on the cooking line. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025
The the drain under the rinse section of the dishwasher is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. The establishment contacted their service company, and all equipment, plates and utensils will be cleaned in the 3-bay sink until the dishwasher is properly functioning. Please contact inspector once service is complete. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2023

The cutting boards in the preparation units are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/26/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Per person in charge establishment is waiting for it to be delivered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/26/2024

The floor grout in front of the ice machine and preparation areas, are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Pictures taken see attachment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024
Some floor tiles in the walk-in cooler are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/26/2024
Meat tenderizer was observed with an accumulation of old food debris at the rack. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023

Corn on the cob and marinara sauce were observed held at between 82- 120 degrees Fahrenheit at the steam unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were reheated at the time of the inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023
Food containers were observed chipped and broken through the kitchen, walk-in refrigerator unit and storage areas. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023

The cutting boards in the preparation units are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023
Food contact surfaces of orange/lemons blade was bent and chipped. equipment was disposed during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following: - Fans at the walk-in cooler main and beverage; salsa preparation unit. - Inside of all refrigeration units. - Corn-chips containers. - Floor at the walk-in refrigeration unit, walk-in freezer unit. - Gaskets all units. All areas MUST be cleaned and kept free of debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023
The hand sink next to dishwasher area is in poor repair (leaking). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023

The floor grout in front of the ice machine and preparation areas, are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Pictures taken see attachment. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023
Some floor tiles in the walk-in cooler are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023
The ventilation hood system has an accumulation of grease that must be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen/storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the hood oven area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023
